      Acetaminophen & 10.554 & 1.427 & 0.5026 & 0.0785 \\
      Ibuprofen & 10.506 & 1.428 & 0.2867 & 0.0819 \\

      \multicolumn{5}{l}{\textit{Train: 28,644 obs\quad Test: 4,287 obs\quad Stores: 93\quad Weeks: 1--399}} \\
      \bottomrule
    \end{tabular}
    \begin{tablenotes}\small
      \item Unit prices standardised to per-100-tablet equivalent.
    \end{tablenotes}
  \end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[htbp]
  \centering
  \caption{Consumer Surplus Loss from 10\% Ibuprofen Price Increase --- Dominick\'s Analgesics (5 run(s); mean $\pm$ std)}
  \label{tab:dom_welfare}
  \begin{threeparttable}
    \begin{tabular}{lcr}
      \toprule
      \textbf{Model} & \textbf{CV Loss (\$)} & \textbf{vs Neural Demand (static)} \\
      \midrule
      LA-AIDS & $-0.2322 \pm 0.0000$ & +2.3\% \\
      BLP (IV) & $-0.2212 \pm 0.0000$ & +6.9\% \\
      QUAIDS & $-0.2339 \pm 0.0000$ & +1.5\% \\
      Series Est. & $-0.2333 \pm 0.0000$ & +1.8\% \\
      Neural Demand (window) & $-0.2383 \pm 0.0012$ & -0.3\% \\
      LDS (Shared) & $-0.2823 \pm 0.0000$ & -18.8\% \\
      LDS (GoodSpec) & $-0.2818 \pm 0.0000$ & -18.6\% \\
      LDS (Orth) & $-0.2407 \pm 0.0000$ & -1.3\% \\
      Neural Demand (static) & $-0.2376 \pm 0.0017$ &  \\
      Neural Demand (habit) & $-0.2151 \pm 0.0006$ & +9.5\% \\
      Neural Demand (FE) & $-0.2425 \pm 0.0020$ & -2.1\% \\
      Neural Demand (habit, FE) & $-0.2222 \pm 0.0007$ & +6.5\% \\
      Neural Demand (CF) & $-0.2266 \pm 0.0015$ & +4.6\% \\
      Neural Demand (habit, CF) & $-0.2140 \pm 0.0012$ & +9.9\% \\
      Neural Demand (habit, FE, CF) & $-0.2234 \pm 0.0015$ & +6.0\% \\
      Neural Demand (placebo) & $-0.2361 \pm 0.0016$ & +0.6\% \\
      \bottomrule
    \end{tabular}
    \begin{tablenotes}\small
      \item Compensating variation via 100-step Riemann sum, $p_{\mathrm{Ibu}}\to(1+0.1)\,p_{\mathrm{Ibu}}$.
    \end{tablenotes}
  \end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[htbp]
  \centering
  \caption{MDP State Augmentation: Brand Loyalty in Analgesic Demand (5 run(s); mean $\pm$ std)}
  \label{tab:dom_mdp}
  \begin{threeparttable}
    \begin{tabular}{lcccc}
      \toprule
      \textbf{Model} & \textbf{RMSE} & \textbf{KL Div.} & \textbf{Reduction} \\
      \midrule
      LA-AIDS & $0.07069 \pm 0.00000$ & $0.02311 \pm 0.00000$ & baseline \\
      Neural Demand (static) & $0.06521 \pm 0.00083$ & $0.02018 \pm 0.00050$ & 7.7% \\
      Neural Demand (habit) & $0.04835 \pm 0.00010$ & $0.01087 \pm 0.00004$ & 31.6% \\
      Neural Demand (placebo) & $0.06656 \pm 0.00072$ & $0.02094 \pm 0.00048$ & 5.8% \\
      Neural Demand (CF) & $0.06835 \pm 0.00067$ & $0.02145 \pm 0.00044$ & 3.3% \\
      Neural Demand (habit, CF) & $0.04836 \pm 0.00010$ & $0.01087 \pm 0.00005$ & 31.6% \\
      \bottomrule
    \end{tabular}
    \begin{tablenotes}\small
      \item Habit-decay $\hat{\delta}$ (habit model) = 0.719$\pm$0.002; FE variant: 0.715$\pm$0.002.
      RMSE and KL: mean $\pm$ std over 5 independent re-estimation(s).
      \item \textbf{Diebold-Mariano Test}: Static vs Habit (blocked by store). DM stat = 8.10^{***} ($p=3.827e-12$). Positive stat favors Habit model.
    \end{tablenotes}
  \end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

      \item Row $i$, Column $j$: $\epsilon_{ij} = \partial \log q_i / \partial \log p_j$.

    \end{tablenotes}
  \end{threeparttable}
\end{table}
